cue: How do you say it's a small world?

es.meaning: It's a small world — said on an unexpected coincidence.
es.example: ¿También conoces a Ana? ¡El mundo es un pañuelo!
es.exampleTranslation: You know Ana too? It's a small world!

it.example: Conosci anche tu Ana? Il mondo è piccolo!
it.exampleTranslation: You know Ana too? Small world!

bridge.note: Same delighted surprise at a coincidence, but only one language reaches for a metaphor. Spanish shrinks the whole world to a PAÑUELO, a handkerchief — small enough to fold into a pocket, a vivid little object. Italian just states the size plainly: il mondo è piccolo, the world is small. A rare case where Spanish is the figurative one and Italian the literal — usually it runs the other way.

interference.es_to_it: There is no handkerchief in the Italian; 'il mondo è un fazzoletto' will puzzle. Italian says it flat: il mondo è piccolo.
interference.it_to_es: Reducing the Spanish to 'el mundo es pequeño' is understood but loses the idiom — the fixed phrase is the handkerchief.

review.notes: ES side discovered in the Spanish idioms book Joe is reading, 2026-07-21 (discovery only; all glosses and examples authored fresh). IT counterpart from Claude's recall — UNVERIFIED. One of the few entries where ES is figurative and IT is literal — worth noting as a reversal of the usual pattern.
